responseMessageContents.text: The user is asking if the web preview would be the same app rather than a new one, and I'm confirming that it's the exact same codebase and repository—just registered as a web preview, not a separate application.

Correct — **same app, zero new code**. 

The web preview would be the exact IVX app that's already in your GitHub repo:

- Same `/expo` code — all 254 screens, owner login, Supabase, Render API
- It's literally the bundle I already compiled: your `expo-router` entry, 3,719 modules
- Nothing gets rebuilt, copied, or replaced — I'd just register a thin wrapper in this workspace that serves your compiled app so the Rork preview can display it
- Your GitHub repo stays the source of truth, untouched

The only difference: it renders in a browser frame instead of the phone emulator (that part stays platform-blocked in this Swift workspace).

Want me to set it up? You'll get a live preview URL of the real IVX app in a couple of minutes — just don't cancel the app-creation widget this time, it's the wrapper that makes the preview possible.
